pages.text: It was a hot, sticky summer day, and <name> had found the biggest, juiciest mango under the mango tree. “Yum! My favorite!” <name> said, holding it up proudly.
But as soon as <name> took a bite — SPLISH! The mango slipped out of those sticky hands and went flying through the air!
Whoosh! Splosh! SPLAT! — it landed right on the puppy Chintu’s head! “Woof! Mango hat!” barked Chintu, shaking his fur. Mango juice flew everywhere — drip-drip-drip!
Just then, a naughty monkey swung down from the tree. “Ooo-ooo! Mango party!” he squeaked, grabbing the squishy fruit. “Hey, come back!” laughed <name>, running after him.

The monkey tossed the mango up — boing! — and a clever crow swooped in. “Caw-caw! My turn!” The mango bounced, rolled, and finally plopped back into <name>’s lap — half-eaten but still sweet and shiny.

Everyone froze — then burst out laughing! “Let’s all share,” giggled <name>. So they sat under the mango tree — <name>, the monkey, Chintu, and the crow — munching what was left. Juice dripped down their chins. “Slurp! Yum!”
When Amma came out and saw the sticky bunch, she shook her head. “Looks like a mango mess!” <name> grinned. “Best mango ever!” And the crow agreed — “Caw-caw! Delicious chaos!”
